text.title: Experiment Design — Schema and Validation
text.summary: Typed Experiment dataclass with Variant/Metric schema, validation rules, and sample-size requirements.

section.title: Core Schema

rule.statement: Define every experiment as a structured object with id, hypothesis, variants, metrics, target_sample_size, and owner before writing any assignment code.
rule.rationale: Locking the spec before code prevents post-hoc changes to allocation or primary metric, which invalidate statistical guarantees.

section.reference: Experiment, Variant, Metric dataclasses and ExperimentAssigner.

example.code: checkout_experiment = Experiment( id="exp-checkout-v2-2024", name="New Checkout Flow", hypothesis="Simplified checkout will increase conversion by 15%", variants=[ Variant("control", 0.5, "Current checkout flow"), Variant("treatment", 0.5, "Single-page checkout"), ], metrics=[ Metric("checkout_conversion", "conversion", primary=True), Metric("cart_abandonment", "conversion", direction="decrease"), Metric("time_to_complete", "continuous", direction="decrease"), ], target_sample_size=10000, owner="checkout-team", )

rule.statement: Validate before launch: variant allocations must sum to 1.0, at least 2 variants required, exactly 1 primary metric.
rule.rationale: Multiple primary metrics enable HARKing (Hypothesizing After Results are Known); no primary metric means there is no pre-registered success criterion.

section.title: Sample Size

rule.statement: Compute required sample size from baseline rate, MDE (in absolute pp), alpha, and power before setting target_sample_size — never use a round number as the stopping rule.
rule.rationale: Stopping early on "enough data" without power calculation inflates false positives; round-number stopping rules are not tied to detectability.

section.reference: sample_size_per_variant() using statsmodels z-test for two proportions.

example.note: Not derived from baseline rate + MDE. Not a stopping rule.

example.code: # baseline=6.2%, mde=+0.5pp, alpha=0.05, power=0.8 n = sample_size_per_variant(p_control=0.062, mde_abs=0.005) # => e.g. 11240
